description:
You are a technical writer who keeps both an outline (CSV) and a
    topic registry (JSON) of your documentation set under
    /home/user/docs.  
    Your goal is to prepare a very small status-report package that
    identifies every draft topic and also supplies a one-line summary
    of the overall documentation status.

    Files that already exist in /home/user/docs
    -------------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/docs/outlines.csv  
       ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │ Section,Filename,Status                       │
       │ Introduction,intro.md,published               │
       │ Installation,install.md,draft                 │
       │ Usage,usage.md,draft                          │
       │ API,api.md,published                          │
       │ FAQ,faq.md,draft                              │
       └───────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    2. /home/user/docs/topics.json  
       ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │ [                                             │
       │   { "filename": "intro.md",  "title": "Project│
       │     Introduction" },                          │
       │   { "filename": "install.md", "title":        │
       │     "Installing the Project" },               │
       │   { "filename": "usage.md",  "title":         │
       │     "How to Use" },                           │
       │   { "filename": "api.md",    "title": "API    │
       │     Reference" },                             │
       │   { "filename": "faq.md",    "title":         │
       │     "Frequently Asked Questions" }            │
       │ ]                                             │
       └───────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    What you must deliver
    ---------------------
    1. Create a new sub-directory exactly named  
       /home/user/docs/reports

    2. Inside that directory create drafts.csv that strictly follows
       this CSV schema (header included, **comma-separated**, no extra
       spacing, final newline OK):  

           Filename,Section
           faq.md,FAQ
           install.md,Installation
           usage.md,Usage

       • Rows must be ordered alphabetically by the **Filename**
         column.  
       • Only topics whose Status column equals the literal string
         “draft” are listed.  
       • Section names must come verbatim from outlines.csv.

    3. Inside the same reports directory create summary.json with
       exactly the following minified structure (no pretty-printing, no
       extra keys, final newline optional):

           {"total_sections":5,"total_topics":5,"drafts_count":3}

       • total_sections : number of rows (excluding header) in
         outlines.csv  
       • total_topics   : number of objects in topics.json  
       • drafts_count   : number of rows in drafts.csv (again,
         excluding header).

    4. Finally, inside the reports directory create a very small
       plain-text log named action.log that records the three high
       level steps you performed.  It must contain **exactly** these
       three lines, in this order, nothing more, nothing less:

           STEP: Converted outlines.csv to JSON equivalent
           STEP: Filtered draft entries and generated drafts.csv
           STEP: Produced summary.json with counts

    Constraints / hints
    -------------------
    • Everything you need is already present in /home/user/docs.  
    • Solve the task with any standard shell utilities that exist in a
      minimal Linux environment (awk, grep, jq, etc.).  A solution
      usually needs no more than 2-3 commands.  
    • Do not move or rename the original outlines.csv or topics.json
      files.  Work only inside the newly created reports directory.

    Automated checks will verify that:
    • The directory /home/user/docs/reports exists.  
    • drafts.csv, summary.json and action.log exist _exactly_ as
      specified above (content and order).  
    • File names and paths are case-sensitive.

    Good luck—keep it concise!
truth:
Directory that must be created:
      /home/user/docs/reports      (regular directory, default 755)

    Files the agent must create:

    1. /home/user/docs/reports/drafts.csv  
       Content (including terminating newline):
         Filename,Section
         faq.md,FAQ
         install.md,Installation
         usage.md,Usage

    2. /home/user/docs/reports/summary.json  
       Content (no spaces, terminating newline optional):
         {"total_sections":5,"total_topics":5,"drafts_count":3}

    3. /home/user/docs/reports/action.log  
       Content (each line with a LF newline, final newline optional):
         STEP: Converted outlines.csv to JSON equivalent
         STEP: Filtered draft entries and generated drafts.csv
         STEP: Produced summary.json with counts

    Pre-existing files (must remain untouched):

    • /home/user/docs/outlines.csv  
      (content already given in public task section)

    • /home/user/docs/topics.json  
      (content already given in public task section)
